examples.title: Dynamic Array Allocation
examples.description: Demonstrates dynamic memory allocation for an integer array
examples.explanation: Uses malloc() to allocate heap memory at runtime. Checks for NULL to handle allocation failure. free() releases memory to prevent leaks.

examples.title: Polynomial Addition using Arrays
examples.description: Algorithm to add two polynomials using array representation
examples.explanation: Arrays store coefficients at indices representing exponents. Addition is performed by adding coefficients at same indices. Handles different degree polynomials by using maximum degree array.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is maximum degree

examples.title: Sparse Matrix using Triplet Representation
examples.description: Demonstrates storage-efficient representation of sparse matrix
examples.explanation: Only stores non-zero elements with their row, column, and value. Reduces memory from O(m×n) to O(k) where k is number of non-zero elements.
examples.timeComplexity: O(m×n) to create, O(k) to store
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) where k is non-zero count
